reasoning: The commodity spot price is generally a random walk in the short-term, establishing a base rate close to 50%. The FRED tool combined estimate gave a 48% probability of an increase, though the purely linear trend projected a lower probability (29%) due to negative momentum following a January price spike. Recent weather forecasts from late February point to a warmer-than-normal first half of March, which should exert downward pressure on prices as the winter heating season draws to a close. Natural gas storage is also reported to be at comfortable levels, reducing the likelihood of a supply-driven price jump. Given the recent slight upward drift to 3.20 (compared to 2.90–3.10 earlier in the month), mean reversion and bearish seasonal fundamentals suggest a price decrease is slightly more likely than an increase.
